key_points: The Constitution is the supreme law that provides the framework for governing India
It defines the structure of government, distribution of powers and responsibilities of institutions
The Indian Constitution was prepared by the Constituent Assembly after extensive debates and discussions
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar served as the Chairman of the Drafting Committee and played a key role in shaping the Constitution
The Constitution came into force on 26 January 1950 and established India as a democratic republic
It ensures Fundamental Rights such as equality, freedom, protection against exploitation and religious freedom
The Constitution limits the powers of the government and ensures rule of law
It promotes justice, liberty, equality and fraternity among citizens
examples: Citizens can approach the Supreme Court or High Courts if their Fundamental Rights are violated
The Right to Equality prevents discrimination based on religion, caste, gender or place of birth
The Constitution divides powers between the central and state governments through federalism
The judiciary interprets the Constitution and protects the rights of citizens
formulas: Constitutional governance → Constitution + Institutions + Rule of Law
Protection of democracy → Constitution + Elections + Fundamental Rights
exam_tips: Explain the role of the Constituent Assembly and the drafting process
Mention the importance of Fundamental Rights and their protection by courts
Write about the principle of separation of powers in answers
Connect constitutional principles with democratic governance
